framing_rules.stage_1: Full body visible, centered, friendly/idle pose. Chibi proportions — oversized head, chubby body, expressive eyes.
framing_rules.stage_2: Full body visible, upright or mid-action pose. More angular and confident than stage 1. Less cute, more character.
framing_rules.stage_3: Full body filling the canvas vertically. Battle-ready stance — rearing, looming, or coiled. Should feel large even at 64px. Tail or lower body can be implied at canvas edge.

style_references.name: Chuffin
style_references.notes: Round body, tiny wings out, huge surprised eyes. Sets the tone for cute/small Flying/Natura types.

style_references.name: Murkling
style_references.notes: Dark silhouette, two white dot eyes only. Template for Umbra types — override outline/shading/detail for shadow creatures.

style_references.name: Cindrel v4
style_references.notes: Charcoal-grey gecko, orange underbelly, low wide-set legs, smoking snout. Stage 1 quadruped reference.

style_references.name: Scaldrix
style_references.notes: Upright bipedal, slate-black with orange underbelly, flame vents on spine, lean and angular. Stage 2 reference.

style_references.name: Pyrathon v3
style_references.notes: Wingless serpent rearing up in battle stance, coiling base, magma-cracked dark armour, orange lava fissures. Stage 3 reference — fills canvas vertically.

Umbra.notes: Shadow/dark creatures use lineless flat shading for that smudged silhouette look.

prompt_guidelines: Always name the Synamon at the start of the prompt
Describe body shape explicitly — do not rely on type alone
Include distinguishing visual feature (smoke, glow, crystal growth, etc.)
End with: pixel art, transparent background
For stage 3: include 'battle stance' or 'rearing up' to get the right posture
For winged creatures: explicitly say 'wings spread' or 'wings folded' — be deliberate
For legless creatures: explicitly say 'no legs, no wings, snake-like body'
